data.title: Peak Rare Earths board endorses takeover proposal from major shareholder Shenghe Resources
data.description: The board of Peak Rare Earths has given its support to a proposed takeover of the company by China’s Shenghe Resources. A partly state-owned Chinese rare earths mining and processing company, Shenghe has proposed to acquire 100% of Peak by way of a scheme of arrangement. Shenghe is Peak’s major shareholder and owns the binding offtake rights to all of the rare earths production from the company’s Ngualla rare earth project in Tanzania. Superior outcome In announcing its support, the Peak board told shareholders it believed that the scheme delivers a superior outcome compared to a previous Ngualla Group UK (NGUK) transaction. The board noted that the new scheme had the potential to provide a significantly higher value outcome for Peak shareholders on a risk-weighted basis. It places an implied value of $150.5 million for Peak’s interest in the Ngualla project, compared to $96m under the NGUK transaction. The board also suggested Peak shareholders would have the opportunity to realise an attractive price for their shares and avoid potential risks around the development, operation and funding of the Ngualla project. “The proposed scheme offer price of a minimum of approximately $0.359 represents an excellent outcome for shareholders and a 199% premium to Peak’s closing share price prior to the announcement,” Peak chief executive officer Bardin Davis said. Careful evaluation Peak chair Russell Scrimshaw said that, having carefully evaluated the NGUK transaction and other alternative options to develop and fund the Ngualla project, the Peak board believes the proposed scheme delivers the best outcome currently available to shareholders on a risk-weighted basis. “We are also mindful of the importance to Tanzania of developing the Ngualla project and believe that Shenghe is well positioned to partner with the government of Tanzania to successfully develop this world-class project.” Under the scheme, Shenghe subsidiary Ganzhou Chenguang Rare Earths New Material has also agreed to pay an amount equal to the funds to be raised under the proposed entitlement offer the company will be conducting over May and June of 2025.

data.title: Venture Corp.'s Revenue Declined Nearly 8% in Q1
data.description:
Venture Corp.'s revenue declined 7.5% year over year to SG$616.6 million, according to a Wednesday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The decline was mainly due to lower demand in the lifestyle consumer tech domain, the filing said.

Net profit came in at SG$55.9 million, while earnings per share declined by 6.8% year over year to SG$0.193.

data.title: Market Chatter: Kexing Biopharm Gets Early Peru Approval for Cancer Drug
data.description:
Kexing Biopharm secured the approval of Peru's drug regulator for its Apexelsin cancer treatment ust two months after submission and five months ahead of schedule, YiCai Global reported Wednesday.

The albumin-bound paclitaxel drug, developed with Haichang Biotech, treats breast, pancreatic and non-small cell lung cancers, according to the report.

The drug previously gained European Union approval in July 2024 and UK clearance in October 2024. Kexing Biopharm has already prepared shipments to Europe, YiCai Global wrote.

Shares of the drugmaker rose 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Emirates Insurance's Q1 Profit Slips; Insurance Revenue Up
data.description:
Emirates Insurance (ADX:EIC) said Wednesday that its profit for the first quarter dropped, while insurance revenue increased year over year.

Profit after tax for the three months ended March 31 was 33.8 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 40.8 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.23 dirham from 0.27 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurance group's insurance revenue was 371.4 million dirhams, compared with 308.8 million dirhams earlier.

data.title: US FDA Approves Roche's Companion Diagnostic Test for Non-small Cell Lung Cancer Treatment
data.description:
Roche said Wednesday the US Food and Drug Administration approved Ventana Met (SP44) RxDx Assay to identify non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer patients eligible for treatment with AbbVie's Emrelis.

The test will detect c-MET protein in patients, flagging them for potential treatment with the c-MET-targeted therapy. The Swiss drugmaker said the FDA's decision was based on data from the ongoing phase 2 Luminosity study.

data.title: Australia's Xero hits near 3-month high on strong full-year results
data.description:
** Shares of Xero rise as much as 4.2% to A$181.19, highest level since February 21

** Stock posts biggest intraday pct gain since April 10

** Stock among top performers on the ASX 200 benchmark

** Accounting software maker reports a 28% increase in FY EBITDA to NZ$638.5 mln ($376.97 mln)

** Co expects total operating expenses as percentage of revenue to be around 71.5% in FY26 with ratio to be higher in H1 versus H2

** Jefferies says expense ratio for FY26 in line with consensus; better than feared because some market participants expected higher ratio from additional remuneration packages

** Citi analysts see potential upgrades to consensus medium-term revenue growth outlook, but expect near-term EBIT downgrades due to higher operating expenses

** XRO up 7.3% YTD

($1 = 1.6938 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Naver Rolls Out 'Now Delivery' Service
data.description:
Naver introduced the Now Delivery service on its Naver Plus platform, enabling one-hour deliveries within a 1.5-kilometer radius using two-wheeled vehicles.

The service highlights nearby stores that offer fast delivery and will soon feature a map-based search for easier access.

The service is initially available at 3,000 CU locations, with plans to expand to 7,000. It operates from 11 am to 11 pm for purchases over 10,000 won.

Broader partnerships are expected later this year, Naver said Thursday.

Shares of Naver fell 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Invest Bank Posts Lower Q1 Net Profit, Total Operating Income
data.description:
Invest Bank (ADX:INVESTB) said Wednesday its net profit and total operating income for the first quarter decreased year over year.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31 was 11.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 82.4 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.00004 dirham from 0.0003 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed bank's total operating income was 62.1 million dirhams, compared with 67.7 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Naver, National Geographic Information Institute Sign Deal to Advance Spatial Tech and Services
data.description:
Naver , the National Geographic Information Institute (NGII), and Naver Labs have signed a memorandum of understanding to jointly improve national spatial information and develop life-centric services that use high-precision mapping technologies.

The collaboration will combine NGII's aerial and three-dimensional data with Naver Labs' digital twin tech and Naver Map's platform for better accuracy and expand data usage. The initiative aims to accelerate innovations in smart cities, autonomous driving, AR/VR, etc., Naver said Wednesday.

Shares of Naver fell 1% in recent trade on Thursday.

data.title: Sunstone Metals Halts Trading Pending Court Outcome on Cleansing Notice
data.description:
Sunstone Metals said its shares will be suspended from quotation on the Australian bourse immediately, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company has requested a suspension until it releases an announcement about the result of its application to the Federal Court about the company not submitting a required cleansing notice under the Corporations Act 2001.

data.title: Fujairah Cement Industries' Q1 Loss Narrows; Revenue Up
data.description:
Fujairah Cement Industries (ADX:FCI) said Wednesday its loss narrowed for the first quarter, while revenue jumped year over year.

Total comprehensive loss attributable to owners of the group for the three months ended March 31 was 35.8 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 38.3 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.102 dirham from 0.108 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed cement manufacturer's revenue was 611.2 million dirhams, compared with 278.6 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: HSBC to Cut 10% of Workforce in France
data.description:
HSBC will implement a voluntary redundancy scheme in France to reduce its workforce in the country by 10%, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing the lender.

The plan, expected to affect 348 jobs, is part of the British bank's strategy to streamline its organization and reduce cost to save $1.8 billion by end of 2026.

data.title: South Korean won extends rally after US-Korea forex talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Korean won up 0.54%, broke 1,400 level Wednesday
  • Dollar wavers on uncertainty over trade deals
  • Concerns over dollar depreciation drive currency realignments

The South Korean won maintained its upward trajectory on Thursday, leading a rally in Asian currencies as talks between Seoul's deputy finance minister and U.S. Treasury officials on foreign exchange spurred bets of prolonged dollar weakness.

The won rose as much as 0.54% by 0240 GMT, marking its third consecutive session of gains. On Wednesday, it had surged 1.8% against the U.S. currency to 1,391.40, breaking through the psychologically important 1,400 level.

South Korea's Deputy Finance Minister Choi Ji-young met with Assistant Secretary for International Finance at the U.S. Treasury, Robert Kaproth, to discuss the dollar/won market on May 5.

This sharp movement echoes the unprecedented rally in Taiwan's currency in early May, when the Taiwan dollar recorded a remarkable 6% surge over two days, clocking its largest single-day percentage gain since the 1980s.

On Thursday, the Taiwan dollar continued strengthening, rising as much as 0.5% to 30.136 per U.S. dollar, reaching a one-week high.

The parallels between the won and Taiwan dollar rallies have fuelled market speculation about potential U.S. pressure on its trade surplus partners for currency appreciation. Taiwan's central bank has denied such claims, attributing recent volatility to foreign inflows and market expectations.

Currency markets found some stability after Bloomberg reported that Washington is not seeking dollar depreciation as part of trade negotiations. Nevertheless, persistent concerns about the U.S. administration potentially favouring a weaker dollar continue to keep investors cautious.

The U.S. dollar index , which measures the currency against six major counterparts, eased 0.2% to 100.9, providing some relief to Asian currencies. The Philippine peso gained 0.3% while the Singapore dollar added 0.2%.

The currency movements come during a volatile week where initial optimism over the U.S.-China tariff ceasefire has given way to uncertainty about the specifics of various trade agreements.

The agreement in Geneva exceeded market expectations, with the United States and China sharply dropping extra retaliatory tariffs imposed on each other.

Regional equity markets remained subdued, with shares in Bangkok and Manila falling 0.4% and 1.2%, respectively, while those in Taipei TWSE:TAIEX and Kuala Lumpur each declined 0.3%.

data.title: Fitch affirms Bulgarian lender UBB at 'A-' with positive outlook
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), May 15 (SeeNews) - Fitch Ratings said it has maintained United Bulgarian Bank’s (UBB) long-term issuer default rating (IDR) at 'A-' with a positive outlook, reflecting strong support from its parent company, Belgium’s KBC Bank.

The lender's shareholder support rating (SSR) has also been affirmed at 'a-' and its viability rating (VR) at 'bb+', Fitch said in a statement earlier this week. The VR balances the bank’s resilient and conservative business and risk profiles and solid financials with its exposure to the risks of Bulgaria’s operating environment.

The long-term IDR is capped by Bulgaria’s country ceiling of 'A-', which is two notches above the sovereign rating of 'BBB', while the positive outlook mirrors that of Bulgaria. Without the country ceiling constraint, UBB’s rating would match KBC’s 'A+' long-term IDR.

Bulgaria’s planned adoption of the euro is expected to strengthen the operating environment for domestic banks, the institutional framework and its resilience, as well as the prospects for continued profitable operations, Fitch said. It should also further bolster banks’ good liquidity position and provide flexibility for contingency planning.

UBB is the largest bank in Bulgaria by assets, holding a market share of about 20% of loans and deposits. Its impaired loans ratio continued to decline in 2024, supported by robust new lending, recoveries and adequate management of problem loans.

While a slight deterioration in asset quality is expected due to seasoning loans, UBB's impaired loans ratio is expected to remain stable at around 2% in 2025 and 2026 as a result of healthy loan growth and the bank’s conservative risk appetite, the global ratings agency said.

Profitability improved in 2024 on the back of increased business volumes and controlled operating cost growth. Fitch projects operating profit relative to risk-weighted assets to moderate slightly to 3% in 2025, before improving in 2026 on cost synergies and supportive loan growth.

UBB’s funding and liquidity remain strong, backed by a stable, granular customer deposit base and ample liquidity buffers, Fitch said, adding that the bank's regulatory liquidity ratios stayed above the minimum regulatory requirements in 2024.

data.title: Julphar Logs Higher Q1 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Gulf Pharmaceutical Industries (ADX:JULPHAR), d/b/a Julphar, said Wednesday that its profit and revenue from contracts with customers for the first quarter climbed year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent company for the three months ended March 31 was 140.9 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 1.9 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.122 dirham from 0.002 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed pharmaceutical company's revenue from contracts with customers was 359.2 million dirhams, compared with 336.8 million dirhams previously.

data.title: Romania's end-March foreign debt at 205 bln euro
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), May 15 (SeeNews) - Romania's gross foreign debt amounted to 205.407 billion euro ($231.237 billion) at the end of March, up from 204.893 billion euro at the end of 2024, the central bank said.

The end-March figure includes 156.881 billion euro in long-term foreign debt, up from 155.005 billion euro at the end of 2024, the central bank, BNR, said in a monthly balance of payments report on Wednesday.

Long-term external debt service ratio came in at 11.5% at the end of March, down from 19.6% at the end of 2024.

The ratio of the BNR’s foreign exchange reserves to short-term external debt by remaining maturity rose to 101.4% at the end of March, from 99.1% at the end of 2024.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Consumer Discretionary Stocks Rise, Materials Fall
data.description:
Consumer discretionary stocks advanced 1.6% at midday Thursday. Australia's seasonally adjusted unemployment rate came in at 4.1% in April, unchanged from March, which led to expectations of a rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia being scaled back.

Embark Early Education said that conditional contracts have been executed for two further centers in Queensland, and the contracts are subject to customary licensing and other regulatory approvals. Settlement is estimated to be in late May for one and early July for the other. Due diligence continues on further acquisitions.

On the flip side, the materials sector fell 1%, as iron ore prices fell.

Northern Star Resources said its group mineral resources increased to 70.7 million ounces, up 9.4 million ounces, while its group ore reserves increased to 22.3 million ounces, up 1.4 million ounces, after mining depletion for the year ended March 31.

data.title: Kimly's Attributable Profit Drops 16% in Fiscal H1; Shares Slip 3%
data.description:
Kimly's attributable profit to owners declined by 16% during fiscal H1 to SG$14.8 million from SG$17.5 million a year earlier, according to a Wednesday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share dropped to SG$0.0119 compared with SG$0.0141 in the comparable period.

Revenue was marginally up 0.5% year over year to SG$159.3 million from SG$158.5 million.

The coffee distributor declared a dividend of SG$0.01 per share for the period, which will be paid on July 15.

Shares of the company were down over 3% in recent trading.

Premium Income of China Pacific Insurance's Two Subsidiaries Climbs in April
data.description:
China Pacific Insurance said the accumulated primary premium income of two of its subsidiaries grew year on year in the period between January and April, according to a Hong Kong Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

China Pacific Life Insurance's premium income climbed 10% to 115.4 billion yuan, while that of China Pacific Property Insurance inched 0.7% higher to 77.7 billion yuan.

The insurer's Shanghai shares slipped 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Gold hits over one-month low; US PPI data in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fed policymakers on hold to seek clarity from the data
  • U.S. PPI due at 1230 GMT
  • Market expects 50 basis points rate cuts by Fed this year

Gold prices hit an over one-month low on Thursday ahead of a key U.S. data, which is expected to provide cues on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path, while a thaw in U.S.-China trade tensions also weighed on bullion's appeal.

Spot gold fell 0.8% to $3,153.09 an ounce, as of 0303 GMT, after hitting its lowest level since April 10, earlier in the session.

U.S. gold futures slipped 1% to $3,156.90.

The United States and China agreed to reduce tariffs drastically and adopted a 90-day pause, de-escalating a potentially damaging trade war between the world's two largest economies. However, global markets still remained uncertain about the outcome after 90-days.

U.S.-China trade truce is good news to the market, so people are looking at risky assets more now, said Brian Lan, managing director at GoldSilver Central, Singapore.

"We are looking at the $3,150 as the next key level. If that doesn't hold, then $3,100 is likely."

The spotlight is now on the U.S. producer price index (PPI) data, due at 1230 GMT, following the softer-than-expected consumer data .

Fed policymakers are leaving interest rates where they are while they try to assess how U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs and trade negotiations will affect prices and the economy. So far, the hard data is giving them little to go on.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell is also set to deliver a speech later in the day.

Markets are expecting 50 basis points of Fed interest rate cuts this year, starting from October instead of July, as previously anticipated. (USDIRPR)

Gold, traditionally seen as a hedge against economic and political uncertainties, thrives in a low-interest-rate environment.

Spot silver was down 0.7% to $31.98 an ounce, platinum rose 0.5% to $980.35 and palladium gained 0.1% to $951.90.

data.title: Hayah Insurance's Q1 Profit Plummets; Insurance Revenue Rises
data.description:
Hayah Insurance (ADX:HAYAH) said Wednesday that its profit for the first quarter dropped, while insurance revenue increased year over year.

Profit after tax for the three months ended March 31 was 535,000 Emirati dirhams, compared with 3.1 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.003 dirham from 0.015 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurer's insurance revenue was 29.7 million dirhams, compared with 21 million dirhams earlier.

data.title: Primega Group Holdings Ticker Symbol to Change to 'ZDAI' >PGHL
